snippet.title: Harmonize - Wewe (Official Music Video)
snippet.description:
In Swahili, “Wewe” means “You.” And in this record, “You” is not just a word it’s a choice, a declaration, and a promise.

“Wewe” is a deeply personal love confession from Harmonize to the woman who holds his heart. Built on S2Kizzy’s warm, emotional production, the song feels intimate like a private conversation turned into music. It’s not just a love song; it’s a man openly choosing his partner in front of the world.

With the line “wewe ndo unanipendeza, dunia inajua,” Harmonize makes his feelings public and unapologetic. He celebrates her beauty, her presence, and the peace she brings into his life. There’s maturity in his words “mimi mwenyewe najipongeza, najua kuchagua” as he appreciates finding someone genuine in a world often driven by money and appearances.

The song also reflects growth. He acknowledges past distractions and people who were only interested in wealth “kumbe walitaka pesa nikawapa moyo.” It’s a vulnerable admission of giving his heart to the wrong places, only to realize that real love was always with “wewe.” Through jealousy, outside pressure, and life’s ups and downs, he makes it clear that their bond keeps rising like the sun after every storm.

The repeated plea “ni bora ningekupa wewe tu upendo, muda wangu…” carries both regret and devotion. It’s the sound of a man who now understands the value of giving his time, loyalty, and full attention to one woman.

“Wewe” captures Harmonize at his most sincere  romantic, reflective, and emotionally open. It’s a royal dedication to true love, reminding listeners that genuine connection will always outweigh money, fame, or fleeting attention.

snippet.title: SUPERGIRL | Official Trailer
snippet.description:
This Summer, find your place in the universe. #Supergirl lands in theaters and IMAX June 26.

“Supergirl,” DC Studios’ newest feature film to hit the big screen, will be in theaters worldwide this summer from Warner Bros. Pictures, starring Milly Alcock in the dual role of Supergirl/Kara Zor-El. Craig Gillespie directs the film from a screenplay by Ana Nogueira.

When an unexpected and ruthless adversary strikes too close to home, Kara Zor-El, aka Supergirl, reluctantly joins forces with an unlikely companion on an epic, interstellar journey of vengeance and justice.

Alcock stars alongside Matthias Schoenaerts, Eve Ridley, David Krumholtz, Emily Beecham, and Jason Momoa.

DC Studios heads Peter Safran and James Gunn are producing the film, which is based on characters from DC, Supergirl based on characters created by Jerry Siegel and Joe Shuster. The film is executive produced by Nigel Gostelow, Chantal Nong Vo and Lars P. Winther. Behind the camera, Gillespie is joined by director of photography Rob Hardy, production designer Neil Lamont, editor Tatiana S. Riegel, costume designers Anna B. Sheppard and Michael Mooney, Visual Effects Supervisor Geoffrey Baumann, music supervisor Susan Jacobs and composer Claudia Sarne.

DC Studios Presents a Troll Court Entertainment / The Safran Company Production, A Film by Craig Gillespie, “Supergirl,” which will be in theaters and IMAX ® across North America on June 26, 2026, and internationally beginning 24 June 2026, distributed by Warner Bros. Pictures.

snippet.title: MEJJA & SCAR MKADINALI - KALE KA UOGA [Official Video]
snippet.description:
Mejja ft. Scar Mkadinali – Kale Ka Uoga (Official Video)

Mejja presents his 1st album: MTOTO WA KHADIJA. 
Known for his witty lyricism, street storytelling and unique Kenyan flavor, Mejja delivers a project that blends Genge, Hip Hop and Afro Urban sounds.
On this album, Mejja not only collaborates with established East African artists but also features new and uprising talents, giving a platform to the next generation of artists shaping the Kenyan music scene.
The album includes collaborations with Jay Melody, Nadia Mukami, Khaligraph Jones, Scar Mkadinali, Dyana Cods, Fik Fameica, Ndovu Kuu, Zzero Sufuri, Toxic Lyrikali, Shee, Moha and Madeh.
Listen, share and subscribe for more great music.

snippet.title: Dune: Part Three | Official Teaser Trailer
snippet.description:
Experience the epic conclusion. Dune: Part Three only in theaters December 18 #DuneMovie #FilmedforIMAX

Directed by Denis Villeneuve and written by Villeneuve and Brian K. Vaughan, Dune: Part Three is based on the novel Dune Messiah by Frank Herbert and delivers the epic conclusion to Villeneuve’s trilogy. The film stars Timothée Chalamet, Zendaya, Jason Momoa, Florence Pugh, Rebecca Ferguson, Isaach De Bankolé, with Charlotte Rampling, with Anya Taylor-Joy, and Robert Pattinson, and Javier Bardem, and features newcomers Nakoa-Wolf Momoa and Ida Brooke.

snippet.title: Victor Ivyic - Joy Is Coming ( Lyrical Video )
snippet.description:
“Weeping may endure for a night, but joy comes in the morning.”
Psalm 30:5
Joy Is Coming is for the moments when life feels heavy, when nothing seems to be shifting, and when silence feels louder than answers. It’s a reminder that what you’re going through is not the end of your story. Pressure doesn’t mean abandonment. Delay doesn’t mean denial. There is purpose even in the breaking..

snippet.title: AKOTHEE - MENOPAUSE  (OFFICIAL MUSIC VIDEO)
snippet.description:
Premenopause is a silent storm many women go through without even realizing it.
The mood swings, the exhaustion, the emotional shifts, it’s not madness, it’s transformation.
But in its silence, it can hurt relationships, confuse loved ones, and leave women feeling alone.
This is a journey every woman will face. And that’s why Akothee is creating awareness  bringing real stories to life, so women can be understood, supported, and empowered.
Because this is not weakness.
It’s a powerful transition.
Let’s talk about it. Let’s understand it. Let’s support each other.
#menopause #brandambassadormenopause

snippet.title: NINAKUSHUKURU - Rev Ruth Wamuyu (OFFICIAL LIVE VIDEO)
snippet.description:
In the middle of practice for the Mambo Sawa Sawa Live Recording, a deep sense of gratitude welled up in our hearts, and this song “Ninakushukuru” was birthed. A simple, sincere cry to God just to say Thank You. If it had not been for His grace, we would not have come this far.

As the final release from Mambo Sawasawa Live Recording, this song is our closing altar — a seal of thanksgiving, surrender, and acknowledgment that it has been God all along.

“Give thanks to the Lord, for He is good; His love endures forever.” — Psalm 107:1
May this sound stir your heart into gratitude, and may your life continually echo with Thanksgiving 

Song: Ninakushukuru (I Thank You)
Artist: Ruth Wamuyu
Album: Mambo Sawa Sawa LIVE
Director: Sir Alex
Production: Zero 2 Hero

snippet.title: Bridget Blue & Nikita Kering’ – Mimi na Wewe (Track 8) | Listening Session
snippet.description:
Captured during an intimate listening session, “Mimi na Wewe” brings together Bridget Blue and Nikita Kering’ in a deeply emotional moment on Rhythm & Blue.

The performance explores a love that feels eternal — a connection rooted in honesty, vulnerability, and the belief that some bonds exist beyond time. It’s a soft yet powerful expression of devotion, where two voices meet as one.

Track 8 from the album “Rhythm & Blue.”



🎧 ABOUT THE ALBUM

Rhythm and Blue is an intimate exploration of emotion through Blue’s lens — capturing vulnerability, love, reflection, and empowerment in their most honest forms. Each song represents a different emotional state, moving freely like thoughts that shift between night and morning.



🎼 CREDITS

Written by: Bridget Blue, Nikita Kering’
Composed & Produced by: J Blessing
